army_special_rules.name: Computational Systems
army_special_rules.text: British Commonwealth units with this special rule ignore the –1 'to hit' penalty for moving and shooting. Units must have this special rule indicated in their Unit Profile to benefit from it.

army_special_rules.name: Automated Recovery
army_special_rules.text: When rolling on the Vehicle Damage tables against British Commonwealth Vehicles that are Rift Units, ignore Crew Stunned results. The damage has no effect other than the normal Pin Marker(s) inflicted for being successfully hit by a ranged weapon.

army_special_rules.name: Keep Calm Lads
army_special_rules.text: Each time a British Infantry unit on a Down Order receives a Pin Marker, roll a D6. On the roll of a 4+, immediately discard it. If it would receive multiple Pin Markers at once, roll for each separately.

army_special_rules.name: Superior Codebreaking
army_special_rules.text: Once per game, at the start of any Game Turn, a British Commonwealth player may choose a Die to draw from the Dice bag, rather than draw randomly. If the Order is issued to an Officer or a Command Vehicle, it may not be used to initiate a Snap to Action! ability. If there is more than one British Commonwealth player, and they decide to use this ability at the same time, roll off. The winner may make use of this ability; the loser must wait for a future turn.

special_platoons.name: Automated Infantry Platoon
notes: The range of the Data Transmission rule on Mk IIC Automated Directors and Mk II Heavy Automated Infantry units is extended to 12".

Mk II Heavy Automated Infantry units in this Platoon may use the Snap to Action! ability as if they were Officers, but may only issue one Order. This must be to a unit within 6" in the same Platoon that ordinarily has the First Off the Line special rule. This rule may not be used in conjunction with Superior Codebreaking.

slots.role: Mk II Heavy Automated Infantry Squad
Mk II Heavy Automated Infantry Squad

rules.name: Friend?
rules.text: 'B for Bertie' may also be taken as a Hero in a United States force.

rules.name: In the Nick of Time
rules.text: When Outflanking, 'B for Bertie' automatically passes the Order Test for coming onto the board

rules.name: Lone Wanderer
rules.text: 'B for Bertie' may never be issued Orders from a Snap to Action! ability.

rules.name: Lumbering
rules.text: 'B for Bertie' has a movement rate of 4" on an Advance Order and 8" on a Run Order.

rift.enhancement: Protection Subroutine. When a friendly infantry unit within Line of Sight of 'B for Bertie' is the target of a ranged attack, Bertie may use this Rift Enhancement immediately after that attack has been resolved. 'B for Bertie' may immediately execute an Advance Order, but may only fire at the enemy unit which made the shooting attack. Bertie does not require an Order Dice to do this, and may use this Rift Enhancement even if it already has an Order Die. If this enhancement is used, exhaust 'B for Bertie's' Rift Die after it has executed this Advance Order.
rift.exhausted: May not be issued Run Orders. HQ Teams

units.composition: 1 Officer in Grade 1 Galahad Heavy Powered Armour Regular: 80

rift.note: Only If Armed With Heavy Tesla Rifle

options.desc: Additional Mk II Heavy Automated Infantry armed with Light Automatic Cannons
Add Close Combat Blades to Light Automatic Cannons, gaining
Replace Light Automatic Cannon With Heavy Automatic Cannon

rules.name: Automated Director
rules.text: A Mk IIC Automated Director does not confer a Morale bonus to friendly troops. When using the Snap to Action! ability, a Mk IIC Automated Director may only issue Orders to friendly units with the Computational Systems special rule.

rules.name: Data Transmission
rules.text: Friendly units within 6" of a Mk IIC Automated Director unit lose the First Off the Line special rule whilst both they and the Mk IIC Automated Director have an Active or Surging Rift Die.

rules.name: Lumbering
rules.text: A Mk IIC Automated Director has a movement rate of 4" on an Advance Order and 8" on a Run Order.

rift.enhancement: Command Relay. When a Mk IIC Automated Director uses the Snap to Action! ability, it may use this enhancement. The Automated Director may issue Orders to up to two units with the Computational Systems special rule anywhere on the table, rather than within 6". If this Rift Enhancement is used, exhaust one of the Automated Director's Rift Dice after it has executed its own Order.
rift.surging: Lose the Lumbering special rule. When using the Snap to Action! ability, the Automated Director may issue Orders to up to 3 friendly units.
rift.exhausted: May not be issued Run Orders. May not use the Snap to Action! ability.

options.desc: Upgrade Rifle with the ability to fire Rifle Grenades

rules.name: First Off the Line
rules.text: Mk I Automated Infantry suffer a –1 ‘to hit’ modifier when shooting.

rules.name: Lumbering
rules.text: Mk I Automated Infantry have a movement rate of 4" on an Advance Order and 8" on a Run Order.

rift.enhancement: High Voltage! When executing a Run Order, a Mk I Automated Infantry Squad may use this Active Rift Enhancement. The unit gains the Deadly (2) and Fast special rules (and loses Lumbering) for the duration of the Run Order. Exhaust the unit’s Rift Die after resolving the Run Order. Note that if locked in Close Quarters, the Rift Die will exhaust after the second round of Close Quarters is resolved.
rift.exhausted: May not be issued Run Orders.

rules.name: Data Transmission
rules.text: Friendly units within 6" of a Mk II Heavy Automated Infantry unit lose the First Off the Line special rule whilst both they and the Mk II Heavy Automated Infantry Unit have an Active or Surging Rift Die.

rules.name: Lumbering
rules.text: Mk II Heavy Automated Infantry have a movement rate of 4" on an Advance Order and 8" on a Run Order.

rift.enhancement: Co-ordinated Strike! When executing a Fire or Advance Order, a Mk II Heavy Automated Infantry Squad may ignore friendly units for determining a Line of Fire. Note that they still require a Line of Sight to their target. If this Enhancement is used, exhaust one of the unit’s Rift Dice after they have executed their Order.

rules.text: Enemy Infiltrators may not deploy within 18" of any force that includes a Cerberus Squad.

rules.text: Instead of HE (2"), the Heavy Anti-Tank Gun has HE (3").

rules.name: Meteor Launcher
rules.text: The Churchill’s Meteor Launcher has the Howitzer special rule. When firing indirectly, it has a minimum range of 36" and a a maximum range of 72".

rules.name: Emplacement
rules.text: The M17 Tesla Cannon Emplacement starts the game with an Immobilised result. In scenarios that do not normally allow units to start on the table, place this unit within 6" of your DZE.

rules.name: Automated Crew
rules.text: An Automated Carrier can never be Pinned and automatically passes any Morale Check it is called upon to make.

rules.name: First Off the Line
rules.text: Automated Carriers suffer a –1 ‘to hit’ modifier when shooting.

rift.enhancement: Firing Solution. When executing a Fire or Advance Order, the Automated Carrier can gain a +1 modifier to its 'to hit' rolls. If it does, exhaust the Automated Carrier's Rift Die after resolving the Order.
rift.exhausted: Loses the Fast special rule. May not be issued Run Orders.
